message:
fix(alloc): recover from PaneArenaRegistry lock poisoning instead of panic-cascade

Three-pass audit of crates/frankenterm-alloc/src/lib.rs (695 lines —
the per-pane arena telemetry crate the rest of the workspace uses for
allocator accounting):

  1. unwrap/expect on startup paths — defect found and fixed (below).

  2. atomic ordering / overflow — clean enough for the current scale.
     `allocate_arena_id` at lib.rs:373 uses `fetch_add(1, Relaxed)` on
     an `AtomicU32` and guards against the u32 wrap-to-0 with
     `assert_ne!(raw, 0, "pane arena id space exhausted")`. That
     assertion will fire exactly once after 2^32 reservations — a
     hard limit that deserves a follow-up (widen to AtomicU64 or
     reclaim released ids) but is a noisy failure, not a silent one,
     so out of scope here.

  3. read_allocator_stats error paths — clean. Every jemalloc stats
     read (`allocated`, `active`, `resident`, `mapped`, `retained`)
     wraps its Result through `AllocatorStatsError::Jemalloc` with
     no .unwrap. The `#[cfg(not(feature = "jemalloc"))]` fallback
     returns `AllocatorStatsError::JemallocNotEnabled` cleanly.

Defect (Pass 1) and fix:

`PaneArenaRegistry` holds its state in `Mutex<HashMap<u64,
PaneArenaState>>`. Every public method (`reserve`, `release`,
`set_tracked_bytes`, `stats`, `get`, `count`, `snapshot`,
`stats_snapshot` — 8 call sites) acquired the lock with
`.expect("pane arena registry lock poisoned")`.

`std::sync::Mutex::lock()` only returns `Err(PoisonError)` when a
thread holding the mutex has panicked mid-operation. In the
frankenterm-alloc telemetry surface a panic mid-op is extremely
unlikely — every mutation is a `HashMap::insert` / `HashMap::remove`
over `Copy` `PaneArenaState` values — but if one ever happens, every
subsequent `.expect` propagates it as a process-wide panic cascade.
Every pane arena mutation across the entire workspace would start
panicking, even though the registry's invariants cannot be corrupted
in safe Rust (the HashMap is structurally safe to observe / mutate
after any mid-op panic, and the values are plain-u64 Copy).

Fix: add a small `recover_poisoned<T>(Result<MutexGuard<T>,
PoisonError<MutexGuard<T>>>) -> MutexGuard<T>` helper that follows
the established workspace idiom (runtime.rs, events.rs) of
`.unwrap_or_else(PoisonError::into_inner)`, and route all 8 call
sites through it. A lock poisoned by an earlier panic no longer
cascades; the caller transparently recovers the guard and proceeds.

Compile-verified: `cargo check -p frankenterm-alloc` finishes clean
in 0.47s; `cargo test -p frankenterm-alloc --lib` — `test result: ok.
26 passed; 0 failed` at /tmp/ft-pane6-target.
